RootLayout.description: Questia gives you one IRL quest per day: motivation, outings, and challenges tailored to your profile and pace. Start free.
RootLayout.ogDescription: Turn your everyday life into a light adventure: short, concrete missions—without guilt-tripping yourself.

OnboardingMetadata.title: Discover your quest style
OnboardingMetadata.description: A short quiz to tune your Questia profile before you sign up — explorer or homebody, planner or improviser.

QuestGenerationPage.metaTitle: How are my quests generated? | Questia
QuestGenerationPage.metaDescription: See the full journey: profile, history, today’s context, archetype choice, AI writing, and safety checks — explained in plain language.
QuestGenerationPage.title: How are your quests generated?
QuestGenerationPage.lead: Every morning, a mission shaped for you: this flow walks from your onboarding answers to the quest you see in the app — with a spark of creativity and guardrails that keep you safe.
QuestGenerationPage.diagramAria: Steps in the quest generation journey
QuestGenerationPage.stepsLoadError: We couldn't load the journey steps. Refresh the page or switch language and back.
QuestGenerationPage.noteBody: Nothing is pure randomness: the engine blends your profile, your history, and the day ahead. AI writes the words; Questia enforces safety, clarity, and respect for your pace.
QuestGenerationPage.privacyNote: You won’t see clinical labels: copy talks about tendencies and rhythm, not diagnosis. Data is used to personalize the experience, as described in our privacy policy.

stepsList.title: Your declared profile
stepsList.body: During onboarding you share how you like to play in real life — explorer vs homebody, cautious vs bold — plus a light personality footprint that steers recommendations without showing clinical jargon in the app.

stepsList.title: Your quest history
stepsList.body: Each completed, accepted, or declined quest leaves a gentle trace. Recent choices feed the engine’s memory — not to judge you, but to understand how you actually move through the world.

stepsList.title: Observed tendencies
stepsList.body: The system infers tendencies from the kinds of quests you’ve lived. It’s a soft read on behavior, not a box you’re locked into.

stepsList.title: Identity ↔ actions gap
stepsList.body: We measure how closely what you say about yourself aligns with what recent quests suggest. That signal tunes tone and intensity — always supportive, never guilt-tripping.

stepsList.title: Your journey phase
stepsList.body: Calibration (near comfort), expansion (small stretch), rupture (bigger challenge): the phase targets the kind of nudge that fits where you are right now.

stepsList.title: Today’s context
stepsList.body: Weather, city, and — if you enable location — whether an outdoor outing makes sense today. When it fits the mission, we may suggest a public place on the map.

stepsList.title: Archetype choice
stepsList.body: From a taxonomy of quest families, the engine picks the best match for your blended profile, phase, weather constraints, and recent quests — so you don’t get the same template on repeat.

stepsList.title: A dash of diversity
stepsList.body: When several archetypes tie, a stable draw (tied to you and the date) prevents always landing on the same storyline. Creative angles for wording follow the same idea.

stepsList.title: AI writing
stepsList.body: A language model drafts title, hook, and mission in a warm narrator voice. Prompts embed safety, clarity, no medical or dangerous instructions, and respect for your chosen language.

stepsList.title: Server-side quality checks
stepsList.body: Automatic rules validate the mission: one actionable sentence, no forbidden content, outdoor place consistency when needed, city mention when GPS is on. If it fails, we retry or fall back to a safe template.

stepsList.title: Your quest for the day, delivered
stepsList.body: One quest is stored per calendar day: reopening the app the same day shows the same mission — no spam regenerations. Tomorrow the journey runs again with fresh context.

how.label: How it works
how.title: Three steps, then your morning quest
how.subtitleStoresBoth: You calibrate in about a minute, then every morning you get your mission. On mobile, everything in your pocket; on the web, the same experience in a browser.
how.subtitleStoresAndroid: You calibrate in about a minute, then every morning you get your mission. On Android, everything in your pocket; on the web, the same experience in a browser.
how.subtitleStoresIos: You calibrate in about a minute, then every morning you get your mission. On iPhone, everything in your pocket; on the web, the same experience in a browser.
how.subtitleWeb: You calibrate in about a minute, then every morning you get your mission — all in your browser, nothing to install.

steps.title: You first
steps.desc: You share your style and what you're willing to try — missions align with your profile and your energy.

steps.title: Profile in 2 questions
steps.desc: Two answers to calibrate the challenge — not too easy, not impossible.

steps.title: Your morning quest
steps.desc: A short mission with a clear ending. Check it off and move on.

examples.title: The Urban Explorer
examples.mission: Spend 1 hour in a neighbourhood you usually just pass through. Photograph 5 things that surprise you.

examples.title: The Strangers' Café
examples.mission: Go to a café you've never tried. Order something you never pick. 45 minutes phone-free — boss fight mode.

examples.title: Your Voice, Once
examples.mission: Call someone you've been meaning to reach. Tell them you were thinking of them. Bonus: really listen.

testimonials.label: Stories & results
testimonials.title: Players who were tired too
testimonials.subtitle: No magic — just small steps when play replaces guilt.

testimonialQuotes.quote: Day 23. I thought I was outgoing. I wasn't. Now I actually am.
testimonialQuotes.name: Sarah

testimonialQuotes.quote: In three weeks I found 8 restaurants, 3 museums, and a new friend — in places I walked past without seeing.
testimonialQuotes.name: Marc

testimonialQuotes.quote: I thought I loved staying home. I was just scared to go out. Big difference.
testimonialQuotes.name: Camille

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitleStoresBoth: What you should know before you start — web or mobile.
faq.subtitleStoresAndroid: What you should know before you start — web or Android.
faq.subtitleStoresIos: What you should know before you start — web or iPhone.
faq.subtitleWeb: What you should know before you start on the site.

faqItems.question: How is a Questia quest different from a simple to-do list?
faqItems.answer: Each morning you get a short mission, tuned to your profile, play style, and chosen difficulty. The goal: a small, doable action in real life with a clear ending — not an endless backlog.

faqItems.question: One account for everything?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Profile, quests, and progress all live on the site in your browser — one account.

faqItems.question: Is the app free?
faqItems.answer: You can start for free to tune your profile and learn how it works on the site. If paid options appear later, they'll be clearly labelled.

faqItems.question: How does Questia personalize my quests?
faqItems.answer: From your profile and the settings you accept, missions stay realistic for you — not too soft, not impossible. You stay in control of what you actually try.

faqItems.question: Does Questia replace a health professional or coach?
faqItems.answer: No. Questia is a motivation and structure tool, not medical or psychological care. If you're going through something serious, talk to a qualified professional.

faqItems.question: Who is it for?
faqItems.answer: Adults who want to move a little more in their week, with light challenges and fun — without guilt. Great if you like games, quests, and concrete small wins.

AppQuest.abandonedSubtitle: No judgment: your streak resets to zero; tomorrow is a new card.

AppQuest.abandonLink: I can't do it — skip this card
AppQuest.rerollConfirmBody: You'll use a reroll and the new quest's XP will be reduced by 25%. This cannot be undone.
AppQuest.reportPlannedHint: “Defer” swaps this multi-day quest for a short quest today and uses a reroll, same as “Change quest.”
AppQuest.reportNoRerollsHint: No rerolls left — you can't defer or change this quest today.
AppQuest.reportMilestoneReminder: Your optional target date is {date}. Today's quest stays short and doable right now.
AppQuest.reportModalBody: Like “Change quest,” this uses a today reroll or a bonus credit. You'll get a short quest you can do today. Pick a target date (within the next {maxDays} days) for a bolder or social challenge — no obligation.
AppQuest.abandonModalBody: That's fine: your streak goes back to zero, with no penalty. A new quest awaits tomorrow.
AppQuest.safetySubtitle: {title} takes place outdoors. Check each point to confirm.
AppQuest.safetyRule1: I stay in public, accessible places.
AppQuest.safetyRule2: I will not enter private property.
AppQuest.safetyRule3: I will check weather conditions before leaving.
AppQuest.safetyRule4: I trust my instinct — if it feels risky, I skip.
AppQuest.safetyRule5: I've told someone my route (optional but recommended).
